name: Kensington Gold & Silver Exchange
description_short: Licensed pawnshop in Philadelphia buying and selling gold, jewelry, watches, collectibles, and other goods since 1982. Offers cash for gold and diabetic test strips.
description_long:
Kensington Gold & Silver Exchange is a family-owned and locally operated pawnshop located in Philadelphia, PA that has been serving the community since 1982. The business is licensed, insured, and has built its reputation over 40+ years of pawnbroking services. According to their website, they were ranked the number one pawnshop in 2018, and they currently maintain a 4.8-star rating based on customer reviews. The shop operates with limited hours, closing on Sundays and operating until early afternoon on Saturdays.

The company offers a broad range of buy-sell-trade services centered primarily on precious metals and jewelry. Their main services include cash for gold transactions, buying and selling of jewelry, luxury watches, coins, collectibles, power tools, and notably, unused diabetic test strips and diabetic care items. They position themselves as offering competitive pricing and claim to pay "the highest prices in Philadelphia" for gold transactions, based on customer testimonials on their website.

What distinguishes Kensington Gold & Silver Exchange is their unusual service line combining traditional pawnshop items (jewelry, watches, tools, collectibles) with a specialized niche in diabetic care supplies—specifically buying unused test strips, sensors, and pens. This dual focus is uncommon among pawnshops and may serve customers seeking to liquidate medical supplies. Their long operational history (40+ years) and stated licensing/insurance status provide legitimacy markers within the pawn industry.

However, potential customers should note that this is a traditional pawnshop with inherent limitations. Operating hours are restrictive (closing at 4:45pm weekdays and 1:45pm Saturday), and there is no Sunday availability. Like all pawnshops, valuations are subjective and may not reflect true market value. The company makes broad claims about pricing ("highest prices") that cannot be independently verified through the website. Customers should verify credentials and compare offers before committing to transactions.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Over 40 years of operational history in Philadelphia (since 1982), indicating business stability
Licensed and insured business, meeting regulatory requirements for pawnshop operations
Ranked as #1 pawnshop in Philadelphia in 2018 according to their claims
Maintains 4.8-star rating with customer reviews praising fair prices and responsive service
Unique specialization in buying unused diabetic test strips and supplies, filling a niche market need
Multiple transaction types available: buy, sell, and trade services for flexible customer needs
Locally and family-owned operation, supporting community-based business
Diverse inventory across jewelry, watches, collectibles, coins, and power tools
cons: Severely limited operating hours (closes 4:45pm weekdays, 1:45pm Saturday, closed Sundays) making accessibility difficult for working customers
Website contains multiple placeholder texts and Yext Knowledge Tag notations, indicating poor quality maintenance and unprofessional presentation
Vague pricing claims ("highest prices in Philadelphia") cannot be verified and typical pawnshops often undervalue items
No online valuation tools, appointment scheduling, or transparent pricing criteria visible on website
Small sample of reviews (20 total ratings) may not be representative; anecdotal testimonials rather than independent verification
best_for: Philadelphia-area residents needing to liquidate gold jewelry, watches, or precious metal items quickly
Customers with unused diabetic test strips, sensors, or pens seeking cash for medical supplies they no longer need
Collectors and enthusiasts buying rare coins, collectibles, and luxury watches at secondhand prices
Small-scale sellers of power tools and used goods seeking to pawn or trade items for immediate cash
diagnosis: Best suited for Philadelphia residents needing immediate cash for jewelry, precious metals, or diabetic supplies who can accommodate restrictive business hours (closed Sundays, early closing on weekends). Primary caveat: like all pawnshops, valuations are subjective and typically below market rates; customers should compare offers and verify credentials before transacting, and the website quality suggests operational inconsistencies worth investigating.
services: Buy and sell gold jewelry for cash
Purchase and sale of luxury watches and branded timepieces
Buying and selling of rare coins and collectibles
Trade-in and sale of power tools (new and used)
Cash payments for unused diabetic test strips
Purchase of diabetic sensors and insulin pens
Buy-sell-trade of new and used goods
In-store browsing of inventory of pre-owned goods
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
